tweets.text: The U.S. and Republic of Korea will increase our comprehensive global cooperation, deepen our robust regional engagement, and broaden our ironclad bilateral ties to face the 21st century’s most difficult challenges. Read the Leaders’ Joint Statement: https://t.co/v4gd7SCKjU

In “The Week at State,” @POTUS hosts a State Visit for Republic of Korea President Yoon, and @SecBlinken travels to Denver for the #CitiesSummit of the Americas to hear from local community leaders. Subscribe to our TWAS newsletter at: https://t.co/gNUp8OngPT https://t.co/QsOgr9NCax

The U.S. continues to enforce existing immigration laws and is working to counter disinformation spread by traffickers and other bad actors, so that people who want to migrate have accurate information about how to do so legally and safely. Learn more: https://t.co/z6YcI3x8dX https://t.co/7mz0lGvEx0

Are you a high school student studying civics or science? Join @‌SciDiplomacyUSA for a Facebook Live talk on “Space Unites Us: From the International Space Station to the Artemis Accords” May 1 at 9 AM EDT to learn how diplomacy supports space exploration. https://t.co/vBmXFeMelZ https://t.co/vCO690sCDw
On #InternationalJazzDay, we celebrate how jazz tells our story, showcases creativity and innovation, and unites people around the world. https://t.co/fMhXsu1W0c https://t.co/PF7O4myKMo

.@POTUS on U.S.-ROK relationship: Our alliance is about building a better future for all of our people. And there’s no better example than our economic relationship and partnership, which is delivering incredible benefits to both our nations. https://t.co/MDJKm3Y8jQ

This week, @SecBlinken attended the inaugural #CitiesSummit of the Americas to meet with local leaders from across the region. From Denver to Curicó and many cities in between, the Secretary’s playlist highlights the region’s vibrant music scene. https://t.co/2vLKzyA49A

Congratulations to the 2023 Greening Diplomacy Initiative Awards winners! The awardees were recognized for initiatives that advance U.S. diplomacy, help conserve natural and financial resources, build climate resilience, and showcase U.S. innovation. https://t.co/OF7JumKrmP

The U.S. is focused on making migration more safe, orderly, and humane, and on advancing the interests of the American people. Learn more about our sweeping new measures to further reduce irregular migration: https://t.co/WbPO1liZFw

Chef Angel Barreto, a member of the American Culinary Corps, was guest chef for the State Luncheon held for the Republic of Korea President Yoon this week.  Chef Barreto says the culinary corps reminds us that food unites us all. @US_Protocol https://t.co/ymdZ0ZkJoD

The #CitiesSummit convenes subnational leaders from across the U.S. and the Americas to find innovative solutions to the issues our cities face, from promoting safe and legal migration pathways to tackling climate change to making sure democracy delivers for all people. https://t.co/X04FzIVnED
Live now! @SecBlinken delivers the Cities Summit of the Americas Closing Plenary keynote address from Denver, Colorado. Also streaming on https://t.co/UUPWq9D82x. https://t.co/FWYMEHKXcB

Cities and states in the U.S. are on the frontlines of confronting the most challenging global issues of our time. At the #CitiesSummit, we’re excited to convene subnational leaders from across the Americas to find innovative solutions that make democracy deliver for all people. https://t.co/QydJkC4lHn

.@StateDeputySpox comments on our active efforts to help U.S. citizens who seek to depart Sudan and our commitment to bringing an end to the conflict in the country, in close cooperation with our partners. https://t.co/a5jZkrgqIU
Live now! @statedeputyspox briefs reporters from the State Department, also streaming on https://t.co/1ToaKbfFQw. https://t.co/8r7ixAW2Yt

"Thanks to our many achievements over the past two years, I can say we chose a better path to a more sustainable world — for us, our children, and our grandchildren," says @SciDiplomacyUSA Assistant Secretary Medina. Read her farewell on DipNote: https://t.co/b1DUjnmtnZ

The #CitiesSummit brings together mayors, governors, and community leaders from the U.S. and across the Americas to ensure that our shared work continues to benefit all our citizens. Learn more about this event being held in Denver this week: https://t.co/W8S14LxEuz https://t.co/Une6fxiIse

#GirlsinICT are breaking stereotypes and driving innovative solutions to create a more equitable and prosperous future. Read @StateGWI ’s latest DipNote on initiatives to increase connectivity globally and to improve digital literacy for women and girls. https://t.co/22gCUjz8Mv

As the #CitiesSummit demonstrates, foreign policy affects U.S. communities in tangible ways, and local communities can positively impact and amplify U.S. foreign policy priorities. Hear from @subnationaldip about why cities matter to our foreign policy: https://t.co/mgLSWX75Jj
What went into planning for today’s state luncheon at the Department? Check out this timelapse video. #StateVisit https://t.co/H8Ao1Xu6we
Today, @VP and @SecBlinken hosted @President_KR Yoon and First Lady Kim for a luncheon at the State Department as part of the #StateVisit.

Live now! @‌SecBlinken and @‌VP Harris host a State luncheon in honor of Republic of Korea President Yoon Suk Yeol at the State Department. Also streaming at: https://t.co/qkQFklMfrk https://t.co/HU3faBmpr5

Live now! @SecBlinken and @SecMayorkas deliver remarks on new regional migration management measures that the U.S. government will implement with partners in South and Central America, at the State Department. Also streaming at: https://t.co/7yEHPH4lMr https://t.co/5JyrNHCYxn
Live now! @SecBlinken meets with @UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres at the State Department. Watch on https://t.co/58Uc9lIgDz or https://t.co/An7h7NGWMI

.@chefedwardlee, a member of the State Department's Diplomatic Culinary Partnership, is the guest chef for the State Dinner for the Republic of Korea. Chef Lee's style of cuisine reflects his Korean heritage & the influence of his New York upbringing and Kentucky home. https://t.co/PvIO77BEhB
🇺🇸-🇰🇷 What critical moments in world history defined the U.S.-ROK relationship? #StateOntheStreet explores this topic in our latest segment. @USEmbassySeoul @USForcesKorea https://t.co/9mejpUu9g9

The United States and the Republic of Korea are celebrating 70 years of U.S.-ROK shared security. Our Alliance extends far beyond the Korean Peninsula today and is now a force for good in the Indo-Pacific and around the world. https://t.co/RdL31kmS0w https://t.co/Vz4kedfa8N

Follow @USGlobalYouth Abby Finkenauer on Instagram as she attends the @UNECOSOC #Youth2030 Forum. This forum is a platform for young people to share their voices for all to hear their experiences and concerns. https://t.co/koIVOyca22

Women innovators around the world are driving scientific breakthroughs, advancing technology, and setting creative trends. This year on #WorldIPDay, we celebrate creators and advocate for more women to protect their ideas through intellectual property. https://t.co/ndrjT9OFPh

The @UN's Black Sea Grain Initiative has delivered nearly 28 million metric tons of Ukrainian foodstuffs to help many of the world’s most needy. The deal also has helped to lower global food prices. Learn how: https://t.co/2Q4KNYkWhQ
.@StateDeputySpox on imposing sanctions and promoting accountability for the Iranian regime’s human rights abuses. https://t.co/gZMY3zgOcS

Today is #WorldMalariaDay. A new malaria vaccine is saving lives in Africa, as countries and international partners work to expand its use. It’s time to deliver zero malaria through investment, innovation, and implementation. Learn more: https://t.co/v0Cby9ZrFq

Republic of Korea President Yoon and First Lady Kim Keon Hee’s visit commemorates the 70th anniversary of the U.S.-ROK alliance, which advances peace and prosperity for both countries, the Indo-Pacific, and the world. Here’s a timeline since 1945. https://t.co/5jO1HVsZAJ

We are working on two urgent lines of effort related to Sudan. The first is to assist U.S. citizens and coordinate with allies and partners to secure their personnel. Second, press for SAF and RSF to implement and fully uphold a ceasefire and allow unhindered humanitarian access. https://t.co/dyn5LXgnii
Live now! @StateDeputySpox briefs reporters from the State Department, also streaming at https://t.co/N9qVImV8rX. https://t.co/ubqNnSYQHq

A State Dinner wouldn’t be complete without a delicious meal — and good company of course. #StateOnTheStreet went to the streets of the nation's capital asking passersby what they would dish up. Fusion, bulgogi, kimchi, anyone? https://t.co/HgOCNOffY4
#StateOnTheStreet is back asking what would make a good diplomatic gift exchange for the upcoming State Visit between @POTUS and @President_KR. What would you suggest? https://t.co/cUA137QFvT

Cities are “where there is more democratic connectivity than at any other level of government,” as @SecBlinken said. This week kicks off the first #CitiesSummit of the Americas, bringing together city, state, and regional government leaders in Denver. https://t.co/Ml1ZQfaSfQ

The United States has donated more than 687 million COVID-19 vaccine doses to 115+ countries. Check out our efforts to share safe and effective COVID-19 vaccines with the world, to help keep us all safe. https://t.co/xBsqzkiUc4

The goal of #WorldImmunizationWeek is to protect more communities from diseases, allowing for happier, healthier lives. We need to act now to restore immunization coverage for the millions of children who missed out on vaccines during the pandemic. https://t.co/A6oyw5YXwF
#WorldImmunizationWeek is a reminder that vaccines are a powerful tool to save lives. Together we can protect more children and adults from preventable diseases. #VaccinesWork https://t.co/Eo1GCCZrzu

.@SecBlinken addresses temporary suspension of @USEmbassyKRT operations and the situation in Sudan: The U.S. will work with all parties in coordination with regional/international partners to bring an end to the fighting and a return to the transition to a civilian government. https://t.co/nhXWWXvbP1

Live now! @SecBlinken holds a joint press availability with Kenyan Cabinet Secretary for Foreign and Diaspora Affairs Dr. Alfred Mutua. Also streaming on YouTube at: https://t.co/kKM19aCMz2. https://t.co/uajUAUNH2s
Ever wonder what goes into preparing for a State Dinner? Ahead of next week’s U.S.-ROK #StateVisit, we give you an interactive behind-the-scenes look: https://t.co/Ye3yXK8PlA

In “The Week at State,” @SecBlinken travels to Vietnam and breaks ground on a new embassy compound before heading to Japan to meet with @G7 counterparts. The U.S. and @NATO also co-host the 18th Annual Conference on Arms Control. For more, head over to https://t.co/58Uc9lIOt7 https://t.co/62mSpX0zwx

Russia is forcibly taking Ukrainian children to Russia, putting them in re-education camps, and isolating them from their families. The world has a moral duty to hold Russian leaders accountable. #TakenFromUkraine https://t.co/pvhnq82IU8
Have you ever wondered how many countries are working together to combat climate change? #StateOnTheStreet has the answer! #EarthDay2023 https://t.co/R1FjMRQfMA

Actions big and small make an impact on the health of our planet. In celebration of Earth Day, #StateOnTheStreet asked people about their green habits. Let us know in the comments, what’s the greenest thing you do? #EarthDay2023 https://t.co/PsBll18mcB

Billions of people around the world have no access to safe drinking water, sanitation, and hygiene. In the latest blog from @SciDiplomacyUSA Assistant Secretary Medina, read how the U.S. is taking action and how you can #InvestInOurPlanet this Earth Day. https://t.co/2J0Ucxtzy4

On #EarthDay, we resolve to build on the progress made over the past half-century and reaffirm our commitment to take actions that will ensure a clean and healthy Earth for future generations. Test your knowledge about our planet's health with this quiz: https://t.co/XxRnntc0h6

We must take action today to ensure a diverse, thriving, and beautiful planet tomorrow. Today and every day, we reaffirm our commitment to protecting, supporting, and healing the Earth. #EarthDay2023 https://t.co/W3ILprRjTe

“Representation matters. Better representation leads to better foreign policy and that empowers the next generation to imagine what is possible.” —Yasmeen Hibrawi, Foreign Service Officer who serves at U.S. Consulate Ho Chi Minh City #NationalArabAmericanHeritageMonth https://t.co/gYhCe4P0Y2

“In my current assignment I have the ability to work closely with Arab states to ensure the UN is providing sound assistance and technical expertise to address urgent matters.” — Ahmed Shama, Foreign Service Officer who serves at the @USUN   #NationalArabAmericanHeritageMonth https://t.co/GDwAIjbcWR

“Working at the State Department, I have the opportunity to support the Secretary and travel around the world with him, where my heritage is not only an asset but it’s an important part of showcasing our diversity.” — Sarah Majzoub, Foreign Service Officer who serves with @SecBlinken

“Visibility of Arab Americans in public service is important because we have cultural & linguistic roots that better assist us in breaking the stigma that Arab Americans continue to face.” — Hassan Abbassy, currently serving at @USEmbassyJordan  #NationalArabAmericanHeritageMonth https://t.co/P8wLut3tWn

“I’m proud of my Palestinian-Jordanian heritage and proud of being an American. As an American diplomat, I share my cultures with other countries.” — Rima Noser, Foreign Service Officer who serves at @USEmbMorocco #NationalArabAmericanHeritageMonth https://t.co/KRzjaIs2NC

.@StateDeputySpox: Reports of ongoing indiscriminate violence in Sudan threatens the safety of civilians and jeopardizes the aspirations of the Sudanese people for a democratic transition. We are very concerned about reports of continuing clashes, attacks on civilians & looting. https://t.co/SSvUwqafnL
Live now! @‌StateDeputySpox briefs reporters from the State Department, streaming at https://t.co/M09ihnSyg5. https://t.co/QrntDgdwv8

#EidMubarak! As Ramadan comes to an end, we send our warmest wishes to all those celebrating Eid al-Fitr. May your celebrations be filled with peace, blessings, and good health. https://t.co/xRCmsMxzlx

When we stand up for the freedom and dignity of workers in the workplace, we strengthen democracy and the economy, and we change lives. Listen to the powerful message of our new @StateDRL Special Representative for International Labor Affairs Kelly Fay Rodríguez. #SpecialRepLabor https://t.co/tnnTs1LJ3m

As we continue to celebrate #NationalArabAmericanHeritageMonth, hear from two Foreign Service Officers on how their experiences called them forward to public service and how they are encouraging others to embrace their identities. https://t.co/9L1tT6mdxF

.@StateDeputySpox: Russia’s obstruction of Black Sea Grain Initiative operations harms global food security by delaying shipments, restricting supplies, and keeping prices high for food-importing countries. Ukrainian grain should reach the world, unimpeded. https://t.co/tLW13GLd0u
Live now! @StateDeputySpox briefs reporters from the State Department, also streaming at https://t.co/M09ihnSyg5. https://t.co/18FnKLPuZI

Baseball and sports are the ultimate bridge between people. Mashi Murakami was the first Japanese player to play in the @MLB: “I stood up and I waved. I was able to just step right into American baseball culture.” https://t.co/xSrDUJHY1U

The sounds of a baseball stadium are familiar to all baseball fans—just as the building teamwork, confidence, and communication is familiar to baseball players, like @therealjguts and Mashi Murakami. #SportsDiplomacy https://t.co/oC7Y8RFbXm

The joy, passion, and fun that baseball fans have rooting for their teams knows no borders. Former  @MLB pitcher @TheRealJGuts: “Baseball in Japan endures for a fan for the same reason it does here.” https://t.co/RrciwqeQm4

Ever wondered how baseball training differs between countries? @TheRealJGuts formerly of @Royals and Mashi Murakami formerly of @SFGiants, sit down to talk about #SportsDiplomacy in the United States and Japan. https://t.co/DfrMnUjlV0
In an @Esquire interview @SecBlinken talks about his love for music, his relationship with @POTUS, and what he’s learned about diplomacy and negotiations from his three and four-year olds.

What do you know about the health of our planet? Test your knowledge about progress to prevent pollution, save endangered species and everything in between with this quiz. #EarthDay https://t.co/XxRnntbsry
Thinking about traveling abroad? Before confirming your travel plans, review the travel advisory for your destination at https://t.co/yR0YJrGtYg. #TravelSmartFromtheStart https://t.co/qsmfDbGoFj

Planning a trip abroad? Make sure you review the Travel Advisories for your intended destination. You can see the world at a glance on our interactive map. Learn more: #TravelSmartFromTheStart https://t.co/ZUIfgAmxZj https://t.co/8P8ltcP2xi
Watch now! At a press conference in Karuizawa, Japan, @SecBlinken discussed outcomes from the @G7 Foreign Ministers’ Meeting and addressed the situation in Sudan. https://t.co/OFfCdrVa1K.

Another example of how critical the Black Sea Grain Initiative is to global food security. Ukraine’s grain is reaching nearly 4 million people in Yemen who are food insecure through @WFP  humanitarian aid and @USAID  assistance.

The State Department joins communities around the world commemorating #YomHaShoah. We will #NeverForget the 6 million Jews and all who were senselessly murdered during the Holocaust and recommit ourselves to fighting antisemitism whenever and wherever it occurs. https://t.co/uG9jXVRnA0

Today we remember the April 1983 U.S. Embassy Beirut bombing and honor the lives lost on that tragic day 40 years ago. As @SecBlinken said, the United States remains committed to countering terrorism wherever it occurs. https://t.co/unbZCqkE4h
This week, the U.S. and @NATO  co-host the Weapons of Mass Destruction Nonproliferation conference. Read about how NATO allies advance arms control and nonproliferation: https://t.co/AlEnVZRiFx

Live now! @‌DeputySecState and @‌NATO Secretary-General @‌jensstoltenberg deliver opening remarks to the 18th Annual NATO Conference on Arms Control, Disarmament and Weapons of Mass Destruction Non-Proliferation. Also streaming at: https://t.co/wLXoIUrNS9 https://t.co/cl8uF4VjfA

Ambassador Taylor is the daughter and granddaughter of Holocaust survivors. As @USAmbHRC, she shines a light on human rights abuses around the world. Read how she and other State Department employees keep their family legacies alive through public service.https://t.co/2QS7VdmsRs

This week, the U.S. will co-host with @NATO the 18th Annual Weapons of Mass Destruction Nonproliferation conference. NATO Allies, Invitees, and Partners are gathering at a timely and important moment to discuss the ongoing challenges in international security and how the NATO Alliance can continue to strengthen the rules-based international order. https://t.co/85W2trpdkc

Before you travel, make sure you check https://t.co/yR0YJrGtYg to access country-specific alerts and advisories that could affect your plans. Learn more: https://t.co/ffqBux5NPo #TravelSmartFromTheStart https://t.co/tcPiMPuh8v

April is Jazz Appreciation month. Get to know Jon Batiste, a jazz musician, singer, and composer, who won five Grammy Awards in 2022. Batiste plays the melodica and several other instruments, including the piano, organ, and upright bass. https://t.co/Nq4HIPJ4SL

The U.S.-Japan Alliance has served as the cornerstone of peace, security, and prosperity in the Indo-Pacific and across the world for over six decades, and we are committed to working together on shared challenges. https://t.co/VT1j0IjX1Z
To the Orthodox Christian community: Happy Easter. May the triumph of light over darkness guide our work to build a world of peace with justice. https://t.co/gegqE6Jee1

The U.S. and Vietnam partner to protect the health of our people. Through @PEPFAR and #COVAX, we address HIV and COVID-19. @USAID's health assistance is addressing current and emerging infectious disease threats. https://t.co/eTbWO80xKV https://t.co/SS3apmjhMy

Nothing ruins a vacation faster than finding out your passport isn’t valid. U.S. passports are valid for 10 years for adults and 5 years for children — but you often need up to 6 months of validity to travel. Check yours today! #TravelSmartFromTheStart https://t.co/0dhHx7uDbP https://t.co/zH9fRY5ZWN

This week, @SecBlinken took part in the U.S.-Philippines 2+2 Ministerial Dialogue and then traveled to Belfast and Dublin with @POTUS. Later in the week, the United States held the inaugural U.S.-Ukraine Partnership Forum. For the latest, visit https://t.co/58Uc9lIOt7. https://t.co/IwVGrXnijZ
The United States is supporting women in Vietnam to become leaders in academia through the BUILD-IT program. Here’s how: https://t.co/st5SKInE7f

Education is a key link between the U.S. and Vietnam. In addition to @FulbrightPrgrm and other exchanges, last fall Vietnam also welcomed the inaugural group of @PeaceCorps Volunteers to Hanoi to support Vietnam’s English education initiative. https://t.co/eTbWO7ZZVn https://t.co/qrqxjN64xK
Live now! @SecBlinken holds a press availability in Hanoi, Vietnam. Also streaming on YouTube at https://t.co/VKuhRgvbDX. https://t.co/RHc59Niygu

The U.S. and Vietnam partner on programs that support countering transnational organized crime, combating illegal unreported and unregulated fishing, and addressing judicial reform. https://t.co/GSOrMKWwy2
Bilateral trade between the U.S. and Vietnam has grown enormously over the past three decades, and bilateral investment is in the billions. Learn more: https://t.co/eTbWO7ZZVn https://t.co/Syy8ZHiSVP

Through public-private partnerships and cross-sector collaboration, we're creating opportunities for investment and innovation. The U.S.-Ukraine Partnership Forum is an example of what happens when we all come together to reach a collective goal. #UkraineBusinessInitiative https://t.co/D5hrtpQoML

.@SecBlinken delivered remarks to the next generation of diverse diplomats during #MSIsatState: Diversity is one of America’s greatest strengths. And here in the State Department, diversity makes our diplomacy smarter, more innovative, and more effective. https://t.co/uD4ambVN8U

.@SecBlinken: The Philippines is the United States’ oldest ally in the Indo-Pacific. For decades, we’ve worked together to promote peace and stability across the region, and we’re growing that effective partnership in scale and scope every single day. https://t.co/KEvt7yLpzI

.@POTUS: No matter what divides us, there are always areas that are going to bring us together if we look hard enough. Standing for peace, rejecting political violence must be one of those things. https://t.co/6LsqugpoHB

@UnderSecStateP @USChamber To learn more about the U.S.-Ukraine Partnership Forum, which is convening stakeholders to support Ukraine’s economic recovery, or to continue watching, visit: https://t.co/QSyiGbTJcn

Congratulations to Special Envoy to Monitor and Combat Antisemitism Ambassador Lipstadt, one of the #TIME100 Influential People of 2023. Her efforts to advance U.S. foreign policy to counter antisemitism contribute to a better world and a better future. https://t.co/xfd7hVtfvK https://t.co/IGyAMtLK6Z

.@BradPaisley performed his song "Same Here" in Kyiv and met with President @ZelenskyyUa. We remain #UnitedWithUkraine.

Passionate about a career in diplomacy, diversity and inclusion, foreign policy, and travel? Our inaugural Minority-Serving Institutions Conference and Career Fair hybrid event on April 14 is for you. Register by April 10. #DiversityatState https://t.co/b4h0ow6Y58 https://t.co/faV2MQhEOG

We are committed to supporting the ongoing political and economic reforms in Ukraine. Through the U.S.-Ukraine Partnerships Forum, leaders from across sectors are working together to create future business strategies and build strategic partnerships. #UkraineBusinessInitiative

In partnership with @MFA_Ukraine, the Department will co-host the inaugural U.S.-Ukraine Partnerships Forum with @USChamber, @CommerceGov, and @USAID to convene public and private stakeholders supporting Ukraine’s long-term, sustainable economic recovery. https://t.co/mLUjDmZB2A
People across the United States are supplying Ukraine with medical supplies and expertise. Read about the incredible efforts by Americans to help Ukraine. https://t.co/Ok5y98ZTRi

Current and future pandemic health threats require the attention of all nations. The U.S. leads by example by building partnerships across the globe, working with the @WHO and the @UN to provide crucial resources to strengthen preparedness and respond to crises. #WorldHealthDay https://t.co/SOUAL3VVfD

Russia’s fraught war against Ukraine has laid bare its outdated, Soviet approach to governance, especially in relation to security. Read how @StateDeptPM is helping countries develop modern and effective ways to provide security to its people. https://t.co/sPH4GU0Ool

Nothing ruins a vacation faster than finding out your passport isn’t valid. U.S. passports are valid for 10 years for adults and 5 years for children — but you often need up to 6 months of validity to travel. In South & Central Asia, 73,744 landmines and other explosive hazards were destroyed in FY2022. Through community liaison teams, U.S.-supported mine action programs engage with local people to determine clearance priorities that strengthen climate-change resilience. #WalkInSafety https://t.co/uMjMOfuOMj

Since 1993, the U.S. has provided over $1 billion to improve human security in the Middle East and North Africa. In 2022, the Anah Oasis in Iraq was safely returned to the local community allowing for cultivation and reducing the impact of desertification in Iraq. #WalkInSafety https://t.co/xCkHJEaLso

In Europe, U.S. conventional weapons destruction programs boost regional security and reduce the impact of explosive remnants of war. In 2022, survey and clearance work granted firefighters the access needed to safely fight wildfires and protect their communities. #WalkInSafety https://t.co/mkqhjdBWr2

In East Asia and Pacific, 125.5 million square meters of land was returned to communities. Through unexploded ordnance survey and clearance programs, tree-planting initiatives become possible and paved the way for a more environmentally stable future. #WalkInSafety https://t.co/1NrzVRoICL

In 2022, 16,739 landmines and other explosive hazards were destroyed in Africa, allowing communities and native animals to walk the earth in safety. Demining opens doors to new economic opportunities and protects wildlife.  #WalkInSafety https://t.co/2Qq9mjEC5P
